assertions.title: My Tickets surface is available to authenticated users

steps.expected: Successful login redirect to /
steps.observed: Redirected to / after login

steps.expected: My Tickets page loads without 404 or blank page
steps.observed: My Tickets page loaded at http://localhost:3100/my/tickets with 5 populated ticket records

steps.expected: URL is /my/tickets
steps.observed: URL is http://localhost:3100/my/tickets

assertions.title: My Listings surface is available to authenticated users

steps.expected: My Listings page loads without 404 or blank page
steps.observed: My Listings page loaded at http://localhost:3100/my/listings with 5 populated listing records

steps.expected: URL is /my/listings
steps.observed: URL is http://localhost:3100/my/listings

assertions.title: My Tickets and My Listings remain protected from guests

steps.expected: Redirect to /login with callbackUrl=/my/tickets
steps.observed: Redirected to http://localhost:3100/login?callbackUrl=%2Fmy%2Ftickets

steps.expected: Redirect to /login with callbackUrl=/my/listings
steps.observed: Redirected to http://localhost:3100/login?callbackUrl=%2Fmy%2Flistings

evidence.network: GET /my/tickets -> 302 redirect to /login?callbackUrl=%2Fmy%2Ftickets; GET /my/listings -> 302 redirect to /login?callbackUrl=%2Fmy%2Flistings

assertions.title: Seeded test user sees populated My Tickets examples

steps.expected: Successful login
steps.observed: Logged in as test@example.com (userId: cmn79wpj90000l0ndjrstscbo)

steps.expected: At least one populated ticket record
steps.observed: 5 owned asset records displayed: Jordan VS Argentina (Pending), France National Team #10 Jersey (Confirmed), Taylor Swift: The Eras Tour (Listed for Sale), Brazil VS Germany (Delivered), Golden State Warriors VS Boston Celtics (Sold)

steps.expected: API returns persisted records with IDs, timestamps, reference numbers
steps.observed: GET /api/user/owned-assets returned 5 records with unique IDs, reference numbers (ASSET-2026-00001 through ASSET-2026-00005), purchase dates, delivery dates, quantities, and prices

evidence.network: GET /api/user/owned-assets -> 200 (5 records with IDs, ref numbers, timestamps)

assertions.title: Seeded test user sees populated My Listings examples

steps.expected: At least one populated listing record
steps.observed: 5 listing records displayed: Germany Away Kit 2026 (Draft), Los Angeles Lakers VS San Antonio Spurs (Pending Sale), Taylor Swift: The Eras Tour (Active), Brazil VS Germany (Sold), Spain VS France (Cancelled)

steps.expected: API returns persisted records with IDs, timestamps, reference numbers
steps.observed: GET /api/user/listings returned 5 records with unique IDs, reference numbers (LIST-2026-00001 through LIST-2026-00005), listing dates, sold/cancelled dates, quantities, and ask prices

evidence.network: GET /api/user/listings -> 200 (5 records with IDs, ref numbers, timestamps)

assertions.title: My Tickets clearly communicates ownership status

steps.action: Review ticket cards on My Tickets page
steps.expected: Each ticket record shows visible ownership-related status or label
steps.observed: All 5 ticket records have visible colored status badges: Pending (yellow), Confirmed (green), Listed for Sale (blue), Delivered (blue), Sold (gray). Badges are prominently displayed next to item names.

assertions.title: My Listings clearly communicates listing status

steps.action: Review listing cards on My Listings page
steps.expected: Each listing record shows visible listing-related status
steps.observed: All 5 listing records have visible colored status badges: Draft (gray), Pending Sale (orange), Active (green), Sold (blue), Cancelled (red). Badges are prominently displayed next to item names.

assertions.title: My Tickets and My Listings are visually distinguishable

steps.action: Compare My Tickets and My Listings pages side by side
steps.expected: Clear visual differences in headings, labels, and metadata
steps.observed: Pages are clearly distinguishable: (1) Different page headings: 'My Tickets' vs 'My Listings'. (2) My Tickets shows 'Purchased' date + 'Total Paid' pricing with 'ASSET-' reference numbers. (3) My Listings shows 'Listed' date + 'Total Value' pricing with 'LIST-' reference numbers. (4) Different status vocabularies: Tickets use Pending/Confirmed/Delivered/Listed for Sale/Sold; Listings use Draft/Active/Pending Sale/Sold/Cancelled. (5) My Tickets shows purchase price; My Listings shows ask price.

assertions.title: Account-center navigation clearly exposes both ownership areas

steps.expected: Navigation menu shows both My Tickets and My Listings links
steps.observed: Account menu shows 'My Tickets' (with blue Ticket icon, position 1) and 'My Listings' (with green Tag icon, position 2) as the first two menu items, clearly labeled and non-ambiguous

steps.expected: Navigate to /my/tickets
steps.observed: Navigated to http://localhost:3100/my/tickets successfully

steps.action: Navigate back to /my, click My Listings link
steps.expected: Navigate to /my/listings
steps.observed: Navigated to http://localhost:3100/my/listings successfully

assertions.title: Account data is scoped to the signed-in user

steps.expected: Authenticated as test@example.com
steps.observed: Session API returns user id cmn79wpj90000l0ndjrstscbo, email test@example.com, role user

steps.expected: All records belong to authenticated user
steps.observed: GET /api/user/owned-assets returned 5 records, all with userId cmn79wpj90000l0ndjrstscbo (matching session user)

steps.expected: All records belong to authenticated user
steps.observed: GET /api/user/listings returned 5 records, all with sellerId cmn79wpj90000l0ndjrstscbo (matching session user)

steps.expected: Only one unique userId/sellerId across all records
steps.observed: Confirmed: uniqueUserIds=[cmn79wpj90000l0ndjrstscbo], uniqueSellerIds=[cmn79wpj90000l0ndjrstscbo], allSameUser=true

evidence.network: GET /api/auth/session -> 200 (userId matches); GET /api/user/owned-assets -> 200 (5 records, all same userId); GET /api/user/listings -> 200 (5 records, all same sellerId)

assertions.title: User-facing history surfaces read persisted pseudo records

steps.expected: Response contains persisted records with real IDs, timestamps, and reference numbers (not hard-coded placeholders)
steps.observed: API returned 5 owned asset records with: unique CUID IDs (cmn79wpjk000fl0nd...), sequential reference numbers (ASSET-2026-00001 through ASSET-2026-00005), real timestamps (purchasedAt, deliveredAt, createdAt, updatedAt), item references (event-stable-001, product-stable-004, etc.), quantities, prices, and lifecycle statuses

steps.expected: Response contains persisted records with real IDs, timestamps, and reference numbers
steps.observed: API returned 5 listing records with: unique CUID IDs, sequential reference numbers (LIST-2026-00001 through LIST-2026-00005), real timestamps (listedAt, soldAt, cancelledAt, createdAt, updatedAt), item references, quantities, ask prices, and lifecycle statuses (draft, active, pending_sale, sold, cancelled)

steps.expected: Records have database-generated IDs and timestamps
steps.observed: All records have CUID-format IDs (database-generated), ISO timestamps, and createdAt/updatedAt fields matching seed time, confirming persisted database records

steps.expected: UI displays the same data as API responses
steps.observed: Screenshots show matching item names, prices, reference numbers, quantities, and statuses for all records from both APIs

evidence.network: GET /api/user/owned-assets -> 200 (5 persisted records with CUID IDs, timestamps, ref numbers); GET /api/user/listings -> 200 (5 persisted records with CUID IDs, timestamps, ref numbers)

summary: Tested 11 assertions: all 11 passed. My Tickets and My Listings surfaces are fully functional for authenticated users, properly protected from guests with callback URL preservation, populated with seeded data showing diverse ownership and listing statuses, clearly visually distinguishable, properly scoped to the signed-in user, and backed by persisted database records (not hard-coded placeholders). Account navigation clearly exposes both surfaces with non-ambiguous labels and working click-through links.
